name: Muhal Richard Abrams
abstract: Muhal Richard Abrams (born Richard Lewis Abrams; September 19, 1930 – October 29, 2017) was an American educator, administrator, composer, arranger, clarinetist, cellist, and jazz pianist in the free jazz medium. He recorded and toured the United States, Canada and Europe with his orchestra, sextet, quartet, duo, and as a solo pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Muhal and I have remained very close friends... So we’re very close friends. ... Muhal is a very special friend
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: one of my mentors, Muhal Richard Abrams formed the, oh what was it – the Orchestra — Experimental Orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I was studying composition with Muhal... I was able to really study serious in-depth composition with Muhal Richard Abrams... When I met him he showed me new ways of doing things... From him I learned more about the history of jazz than anything else.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I'd go to Muhal who gave me advice about music, about life and about helping me get over my fear of coming to New York, going to New York.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: Muhal Richard Abrams is going to try to predict the future of the music. Each one of us will do about 25 to 30 minutes worth of music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: that’s actually where Muhal Richard Abrams was the guest conductor and we did a concert with him playing his music.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022
